name: Stephanie Kifowit
bio: Stephanie Kifowit is a U.S. Marine Corps veteran, former registered financial advisor, and Illinois State Representative for District 84 (Oswego/Aurora area), currently running for Illinois Comptroller in the March 17, 2026 Democratic primary. Kifowit grew up the daughter of a single mother in a blended working-class family in the western suburbs. She joined the Marine Corps at age 17, right out of high school, and served from 1990 to 1994, including an overseas deployment. After her service, she worked multiple jobs while earning both a B.S. in Political Science and an M.P.A. (Master of Public Administration) from Northern Illinois University, DeKalb. She then applied her expertise as a registered financial advisor, helping families plan for retirement and investments. She served as an Alderman for the City of Aurora for approximately ten years before winning election to the Illinois House of Representatives in 2012 (District 84 — Oswego/Aurora). She has served continuously since January 9, 2013 and her current term runs through January 13, 2027. In Springfield, she has consistently served on budget and appropriations committees, including through the 2½-year state budget impasse under Gov. Rauner. Her 2025–2026 committee assignments include chair of Appropriations-Pensions and Personnel, the Personnel & Pensions Committee, Veterans' Affairs Committee, and the Appropriations-General Service Committee. She is the 2024 and 2025 'Legislator of the Year' recipient from the Illinois Association of County Veterans Assistance Commissions. She is running for Comptroller citing her unique combination of financial advisory credentials, 20+ years of state budget experience, and familiarity with the Comptroller's office staff and the $53M modernization act already underway.
prior_office: Illinois State Representative, District 84 (2013–present); Alderman, City of Aurora (approx. 2003–2012)

related_committees.name: Friends of Stephanie Kifowit
related_committees.relationship: Officers with same name; primary campaign committee

jacket.note: ILSBE Committee 40807 (Kifowit for Comptroller): $99,503 available as of Dec 31, 2025; $137,669 contributions since Dec 31, 2025; $237,172 cash on hand; $20,000 outstanding debts. Combined with Friends of Stephanie Kifowit (ID 20034, $49,086 COH) total ILSBE funds approximately $286,258. Third in fundraising among the four Comptroller Democratic candidates per media reports.
jacket.donors_note: Itemized donor data available via JS-rendered ILSBE page but not extractable via web fetch. Individual donor detail pending browser-based pull from committees/40807/ and committees/20034/.

key_votes.bill: Illinois FY2023 Budget — Appropriations-General Service Committee
key_votes.summary: Kifowit served as Vice Chair of the Appropriations-General Service Committee during budget deliberations. Specific vote details require ILGA roll call review, but her ongoing assignment to this committee spanning multiple administrations is her signature legislative role.

Kifowit chaired the Veterans' Affairs Committee for multiple consecutive terms (2017–2026). She was named 'Legislator of the Year' by the Illinois Association of County Veterans Assistance Commissions in both 2024 and 2025 for her advocacy for veterans services and military sexual trauma victims.
Chaired the IL House Government Transparency Committee in 2017, consistent with her stated emphasis on fiscal accountability and transparent government operations.

Kifowit highlights her familiarity with the $53M Comptroller modernization act as evidence of her readiness. Her years on appropriations committees mean she has reviewed the Comptroller's budget almost every year as a legislator, giving her institutional knowledge of the office.

endorsements: Roofers, Waterproofers and Allied Workers Local 11
National Association of Letter Carriers Branch 219
Illinois Federation of Public Employees Local 4408

career_history.highlight: 13 consecutive years (6+ terms). Chaired Veterans' Affairs Committee and Government Transparency Committee. Consistently assigned to Appropriations. 2024–2025 Legislator of the Year, IL Association of County Veterans Assistance Commissions.
Approximately 10 years of municipal executive experience representing Aurora ward constituents before state legislative career.
Worked in banking and as a registered financial advisor helping families with retirement and investment planning — her primary financial credentials claim.
Enlisted at age 17, served through 1994 including overseas deployment. Honorable service.
Worked as substitute teacher and in banking/financial advisory while working through college.

policy_platform.position: Expand current Prevailing Wage Division into a full Labor Division to conduct proactive pre-payment audits of all Illinois labor laws, including state OSHA standards, to catch violations before taxpayer dollars go out the door. Codify Comptroller's Executive Order 19-01 into law.
Create a dedicated division to address bureaucratic delays in state payments to nonprofits and social service providers, with a public-facing dashboard showing grant payment status in real-time.

Continue $53M Comptroller modernization initiative: invest in modern ERP systems, implement advanced data analytics for fraud detection, create user-friendly digital portals for vendors and local governments.
Protect victims of military sexual trauma; advocate for veterans services. As the only female veteran in the comptroller race, frames veterans' advocacy as a core priority.
Wants to increase state reserves and cut spending that benefits corporations rather than working families. Opposed to raising taxes on the middle class.

social_pulse.summary: Active in western suburbs and veteran communities. Strong labor union endorsement network. Positive sentiment among veterans' advocacy groups. Runs competitively as an outsider to the Democratic legislative establishment despite being a 13-year incumbent — pitches herself as the most technically qualified candidate in the field.

2024 and 2025 Legislator of the Year — IL Association of County Veterans Assistance Commissions — confirmed
